reasoning:
Arjun Erigaisi's Wikipedia FIDE rating dropped significantly by February 28 to 2745 (down from 2775), reflecting a substantial rating loss at the Tata Steel Masters 2026 (completed early February, where he scored 4.5/13 against strong opposition, losing ~30 Elo). According to the text fetched from the Wikipedia page natively as of March 1, 2026, his exact listing is 2745. Consequently, the threshold to resolve YES requires him to have a listed Elo rating of >= 2772.45 on the given resolution dates. 

On March 8, the listing is virtually guaranteed to remain unchanged at 2745, as FIDE updates classical chess ratings on a strict monthly basis. On March 31, if the page has not updated to the April list, the rating persists at 2745; if it does update a day early to the April list, he would need to have gained approximately 28 Elo in a single month of classical tournament play. At the super-GM level, a +28 point turnaround in exactly one month requires phenomenal and extremely rare 2900+ performances over a high volume of games. Because the baseline of 2745 is accurate and monthly chess rating shifts of this magnitude are extremely uncommon, an increase meeting the 1% condition is highly improbable. Probabilities are set to the minimum constrained bound plus a slight increase for horizon.
